# Tardigrades

Tardigrades, also called water bears, are microscopic eight-legged animals.
They were first described by the German zoologist Johann August Ephraim Goeze
in 1773. Most tardigrades are between 0.3 and 0.5 millimeters long.

Tardigrades are extremophiles. They can enter a dehydrated state called a tun,
in which their metabolism drops to less than 0.01 percent of normal. In the tun
state they have survived temperatures near absolute zero, pressures greater than
the deepest ocean trenches, and the vacuum of outer space. In 2007 they became
the first animals known to survive direct exposure to space.
